issues.body: Since round 1, this PR has gained a very large generated benchmark diff that changes the latest commit, nearly every displayed measurement, and extensive history. Many values move by roughly 2x or more while one string-filter benchmark changes by an order of magnitude, indicating a different runner/build state rather than a deterministic consequence of the CSS/component work. Landing this would publish non-canonical performance data and obscure the actual design-system change; the diff provides no provenance or ratchet evidence establishing that this regenerated snapshot is valid.
issues.fix_hint: Remove the generated benchmark-data changes from this PR. If a refresh is intentional, submit it as a dedicated change produced by the canonical benchmark pipeline, with the source commit/runner provenance and required performance-ratchet validation.
issues.title: Unrelated benchmark regeneration replaces the site's published performance data

verdict.summary: Round 2 regresses from the previously approved, narrowly scoped design-system change by adding a wholesale regeneration of the public benchmark dataset. The generated snapshot is unrelated to the Badge/Button accessibility synchronization and replaces the latest result plus extensive history with measurements from different commits, including broad multi-fold timing changes. This is not reviewable as a harmless generated-file refresh in this PR and violates the repository rule that session/work-box timings are non-canonical. The UI changes themselves remain internally consistent, but the benchmark churn must be removed or submitted separately with provenance and the benchmark ratchet/generation evidence.
